examples.title: Sequential File Search Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates linear search in a sequential file
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SequentialSearch(file, key)
BEGIN
 OPEN file FOR READ
 WHILE NOT EOF(file) DO
 READ record FROM file
 IF record.key == key THEN
 CLOSE file
 RETURN record
 END IF
 END WHILE
 CLOSE file
 RETURN NOT FOUND
END
examples.explanation: Reads file from beginning until key is found or EOF reached. Time complexity O(n) in worst case.

examples.title: Indexed File Search Algorithm
examples.description: Two-level search using index for fast access
examples.code:
PROCEDURE IndexedSearch(file, index, key)
BEGIN
 // Step 1: Search index
 binarySearch(index, key, low, high)
 blockAddress <- index[high].address
 
 // Step 2: Read relevant data block
 SEEK file TO blockAddress
 READ block FROM file
 
 // Step 3: Search within block
 FOR each record IN block DO
 IF record.key == key THEN
 RETURN record
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN NOT FOUND
END
examples.explanation: Uses binary search on index (O(log n)) to find block, then linear search within block.

examples.title: Hash Function for Direct Access
examples.description: Simple hash function for file record addressing
examples.code:
FUNCTION Hash(key, bucketCount)
BEGIN
 // Simple modulo hash
 hashValue <- key MOD bucketCount
 RETURN hashValue
END

PROCEDURE DirectAccess(file, key)
BEGIN
 bucket <- Hash(key, file.bucketCount)
 SEEK file TO bucket * bucketSize
 READ record FROM file
 IF record.key == key THEN
 RETURN record
 ELSE
 RETURN NOT FOUND
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: Computes bucket address using hash function. Average case O(1), worst case O(n) with collisions.
